program.name: National School Lunch Program
program.description_short: Free or reduced-price school meals for eligible Kansas students
program.description:
The National School Lunch Program gives free or lower-cost meals to students at Kansas schools that take part.

Students from families with low incomes get free meals. Families with slightly higher incomes get lower-cost meals. Children get free meals right away if their family gets SNAP, FDPIR, or TANF benefits. Foster children, homeless children, runaway children, migrant children, and children in Head Start also get free meals right away.

The program is open to all students at schools that take part. Anyone who qualifies can get school meals, no matter their legal or immigration papers.

To apply, contact your child's school or school district to get a paper form. You can also visit the KSDE School Meals Application website to apply online. Many Kansas school districts offer online forms. You can apply at any time during the school year, and meals start as soon as you are approved.
program.website_description: Free or reduced-price school meals for eligible Kansas students

documents.text: Completed meal benefits application (available from your school or online at schoolmealsapp.ksde.org)
Proof of household income for all members (ex: pay stubs, tax returns, employer letter)
Case number for SNAP, FDPIR, or TANF (if applicable — waives income verification)
Last four digits of the Social Security Number of the adult household member signing the application (or a statement that no SSN has been assigned)
